description: Perform a psychometric evaluation of the Big Five Inventory (BFI) using the `psych::bfi` dataset (2800 subjects, 25 items). Reverse-code the negatively worded items A1, C4, C5, E1, E2, O2, and O5. Calculate Cronbach's alpha for each of the five subscales (A, C, E, N, O), fit a Graded Response Model (GRM) to all 25 items, and compute item fit statistics (S-X2). Save the results to /home/ga/RProjects/output/ including `bfi_reliability.csv` (with columns `scale` and `alpha`), `bfi_grm_parameters.csv` (containing IRT parameters for all items with columns for item name, discrimination `a`, and thresholds), `bfi_item_fit.csv` (item fit statistics), and `bfi_irt_plots.png` (a multi-panel visualization including Test Information Function and Item Characteristic Curves).
